prose:
The fukui-reactivity for phenol was calculated using Density Functional Theory (DFT) with the B3LYP functional and def2-TZVP basis set (chemkit tier `standard`). The calculation was performed in the gas phase with a charge of 0 and a multiplicity of 1, using PySCF as the backend. The geometry was taken from the provided `/home/erobles/chem-skills/benchmarks/fidelity/fukui-reactivity-validation/phenol/phenol.xyz` file and was not optimized as part of this calculation. The SCF converged successfully.

The most electrophilic site (highest f+ value) is carbon atom 3 (0-indexed atom 2, symbol 'C') with a Fukui f+ index of 2.1028.
The most nucleophilic site (highest f- value) is oxygen atom 1 (0-indexed atom 0, symbol 'O') with a Fukui f- index of 0.16201.

All fukui indices are listed below:
Fukui f+ indices (electrophilic attack, N-1 state):
[-0.99514 (O), 0.39933 (C), 2.10283 (C), 1.61968 (C), -0.34201 (C), -0.28911 (C), 1.30798 (C), -0.73392 (H), -0.27466 (H), -0.91706 (H), -0.62992 (H), -0.29592 (H), 0.04790 (H)]

Fukui f- indices (nucleophilic attack, N+1 state):
[0.16201 (O), 0.04877 (C), 0.07097 (C), 0.07050 (C), 0.02670 (C), 0.02095 (C), 0.12352 (C), 0.08232 (H), 0.08815 (H), 0.08111 (H), 0.08049 (H), 0.09350 (H), 0.05101 (H)]
